close_reason: split_path returns Result<_, SplitPathError> — no re-scan on error path
description:
src/lib.rs:98 'parsed.path().bytes().filter(|&b| b == b'/').count() > 1' re-scans the entire path string inside the error builder. split_path already determined whether the slash count is the issue (via 'secret.contains('/')') but threw that information away by returning Option.

Cost: minor, only paid on the error path, but the redundancy is also a maintainer trap — the two slash-checking sites can drift.

Fix: change split_path to return Result<(_, _), SplitPathError> with variants {NoSlash, EmptyVault, EmptySecret, NestedSecret}, and let from_uri match on the variant to produce the right error message. Single source of truth.
title: error path re-scans path bytes to count slashes; redundant work
